# Cerememory

> A neuroscience-inspired memory database that gives AI systems persistent, evolving memory. LLM-agnostic. User-sovereign. Brain-inspired.

## Getting Started

- [Introduction](https://cerememory.dev/docs/getting-started/introduction): Learn what Cerememory is and how it gives AI systems persistent, living memory.
- [Installation](https://cerememory.dev/docs/getting-started/installation): Install Cerememory via cargo, Docker, pip, or npm.
- [Quick Start](https://cerememory.dev/docs/getting-started/quick-start): Store, recall, and forget your first memory with Cerememory.

## Architecture

- [Architecture Overview](https://cerememory.dev/docs/architecture/overview): Understand Cerememory's layered architecture and crate structure.
- [Five Memory Stores](https://cerememory.dev/docs/architecture/five-stores): Detailed explanation of Cerememory's five brain-inspired memory stores.
- [Living Dynamics](https://cerememory.dev/docs/architecture/living-dynamics): How memories decay, accumulate noise, respond to emotion, and evolve over time.
- [Data Model](https://cerememory.dev/docs/architecture/data-model): Core data structures: MemoryRecord, FidelityState, EmotionVector, and more.

## Protocol

- [CMP Protocol Overview](https://cerememory.dev/docs/protocol/overview): Introduction to the Cerememory Protocol (CMP) -- the transport-agnostic API for living memory.
- [Encode Operations](https://cerememory.dev/docs/protocol/encode): Store and update memories using CMP encode operations.
- [Recall Operations](https://cerememory.dev/docs/protocol/recall): Query and retrieve memories using multimodal cues, association traversal, timelines, graph subgraphs, and raw journal forensic recall.
- [Lifecycle Operations](https://cerememory.dev/docs/protocol/lifecycle): Manage memory dynamics: consolidation, decay, mode switching, forgetting, export, and import.
- [Introspect Operations](https://cerememory.dev/docs/protocol/introspect): Observe system state, inspect records, forecast decay, and monitor evolution.

## Sdk

- [Python SDK](https://cerememory.dev/docs/sdk/python): Reference for the Cerememory Python SDK with sync and async clients.
- [TypeScript SDK](https://cerememory.dev/docs/sdk/typescript): Reference for the Cerememory TypeScript/JavaScript SDK.
- [Native Bindings](https://cerememory.dev/docs/sdk/native-bindings): In-process PyO3 and napi-rs bindings for Python and Node.js.

## Deployment

- [Configuration](https://cerememory.dev/docs/deployment/configuration): Configure Cerememory via TOML file, environment variables, and CLI flags.
- [Docker](https://cerememory.dev/docs/deployment/docker): Run Cerememory with Docker and Docker Compose.
- [Kubernetes](https://cerememory.dev/docs/deployment/kubernetes): Deploy Cerememory to Kubernetes with probes, persistent storage, and scaling.

## Reference

- [API Endpoints](https://cerememory.dev/docs/reference/api-endpoints): Complete HTTP REST endpoint reference for the Cerememory Protocol.
- [CLI Reference](https://cerememory.dev/docs/reference/cli): Complete reference for all Cerememory CLI commands and flags.
- [LLM Adapters](https://cerememory.dev/docs/reference/llm-adapters): Configure and use Claude, GPT, and Gemini adapters for embedding, summarization, and relation extraction.

## Contributing

- [Contributing Guide](https://cerememory.dev/docs/contributing/guide): How to contribute to Cerememory: PR process, commit conventions, and code style.
- [Development Setup](https://cerememory.dev/docs/contributing/development): Set up your local development environment for Cerememory development.
